mission.title: open-policy-agent-opa-: Load multiple policy bundles
mission.description: Load multiple policy bundles. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-policy-agent-opa- deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-policy-agent-opa- version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
helm list -n open-policy-agent-opa- 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-policy-agent-opa- installation.

steps.title: Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-
```
I'm trying to find a way to load policies from different bundles into the same OPA instance. So far I was unable to do so as you can only specify 1 bundle in the config file (https://www.openpolicyagent.org/docs/bundles.html).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Load multiple policy bundles
steps.description:
This change brings in support for multiple bundles to be downloaded
and activated OPA.

This is enabled by using the new config option `bundles` to define
the bundles, and deprecates the older `bundle` option.

The new `bundles` keyword and structure is propagated through to the
decision logs,
```yaml
services:
  acmecorp:
    url: https://example.com/service/v1

bundle:
  name: authz/bundle.tar.gz
  prefix: somedir
  service: acmecorp
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-
kubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Load multiple policy bundles" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This change brings in support for multiple bundles to be downloaded
and activated OPA.

This is enabled by using the new config option `bundles` to define
the bundles, and deprecates the older `bundle` option.

The new `bundles` keyword and structure is propagated through to the
decision logs, status API, provenance, stored manifests, etc. Check
out the doc changes for all the updated structures.
codeSnippets:
services:
  acmecorp:
    url: https://example.com/service/v1

bundle:
  name: authz/bundle.tar.gz
  prefix: somedir
  service: acmecorp

services:
  acmecorp:
    url: https://example.com/service/v1
  hooli:
    url: https://hooli.com/service/

bundle:
  name: authz/bundle.tar.gz
  prefix: somedir
  service: acmecorp

services:
  acmecorp:
    url: https://example.com/service/v1
  hooli:
    url: https://hooli.com/service/

bundles:
  authz/bundle.tar.gz:
    resource: somedir/authz/bundle.tar.gz
    service: acmecorp
  hooli-bundle:
    resource: somedir/authz/bundle.tar.gz
    service: hooli

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-policy-agent-opa- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
